Name: 2_united_arsenal_villa_chelsea
Representative_Docs: ['Doku, Bobb, Stones, Rodri, Kovacic latest: Man City injury list for West Ham clash By Oliver Thomas | 18 Dec 2025 08:05, Last updated: 18 Dec 2025 08:09 Manchester City will be looking to win their seventh game in a row in all competitions when they welcome West Ham United to the Etihad Stadium for their latest Premier League fixture on Saturday afternoon. The Citizens are in high spirits after securing their spot in the semi-finals of the EFL Cup with a 2-0 home victory over Brentford on Wednesday night, Prior to that triumph, Pep Guardiola’s men beat Crystal Palace by a 3-0 scoreline in the Premier League to climb up to second place in the table and move to within two points of leaders Arsenal. Here, Sports Mole rounds up Man City’s latest injury and suspension news ahead of the contest against the Hammers. Status: Major doubt Type of injury: Hamstring Possible return date: Unknown Oscar Bobb was forced off with an apparent hamstring injury in the EFL Cup win over Brentford and it remains to be seen whether he will be facing a period on the sidelines. Status: Out Type of injury: Leg Possible return date: January 1 (vs. Sunderland) Jeremy Doku has missed Man City’s last two games with a leg injury and Guardiola expects the winger to be sidelined for the next two', "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Pressure was piling on Arne Slot after the Reds slumped to ninth in the table, but even without a number of key players, the champions were able to sweep Villa aside to move back up to third. They welcome Real Madrid - Trent Alexander-Arnold, Xabi Alonso, et al - to Anfield in midweek, before they take on Manchester City in a mouth-watering encounter at the weekend. But will they have any of their star names back from injury? Ahead of a big few weeks, here’s all the latest Liverpool injury news and return dates... Alexander Isak has endured a tough start to life on Merseyside. Subbed at half-time in Liverpool’s 5-1 win over Frankfurt in the Champions League, Slot later revealed the £125million summer signing had suffered a minor groin injury. “Alexander had to go off at half-time because he felt his groin a little bit. That's a pity,” said Slot. “I've said many times it is such a difficult balance to find with a player that missed three months.” Isak was not in the squad in the 3-2 loss at Brentford, and he also missed out on the Carabao Cup defeat agai", "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Sport | Football Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Name: 3_passed away_anthony_memorial memory_living memorial
Representative_Docs: ['Anthony Duarte Birth: 1980 Death: 2025 Anthony Duarte, of La Puente, California, passed away on Wednesday, October 29, 2025, surrounded by love and family. Born on June 9, 1980, in West Covina, California, to Doreen Cardenas and Gildardo Duarte, Anthony was raised with deep love and guidance, and shared a profoundly special bond with his father Robert Cardenas, who helped shape the man he became. Anthony lived a life marked by warmth, creativity, and generosity. His kindness was as constant as his humor, and he approached every day with an open heart and an unwavering sense of purpose. Anthony’s passions reflected his deep belief in connection — through both community and service. He had a profound dedication to civic life, demonstrated by his years of service on the Hacienda La Puente Unified School Board, his time working for a member of Congress, and his contributions to the California Democratic Party. Through these roles, he championed education, representation, and opportunity for others, always guided by empathy and integrity. Anthony cared deeply for his friends and made a lasting impact on everyone lucky enough to know him. He was the first to reach out when someone needed support and al', 'Anthony Duarte Birth: 1980 Death: 2025 Anthony Duarte, of La Puente, California, passed away on Wednesday, October 29, 2025, surrounded by love and family. Anthony cared deeply for his friends and made a lasting impact on everyone lucky enough to know him. He was the first to reach out when someone needed support and al', 'John Kevin King Birth: 1956 Death: 2025 John Kevin King of La Habra Heights, California, passed away peacefully on November 5, 2025. He was born in Tulsa, Oklahoma, and was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, and friend. John earned his masters degree in civil engineering from Oklahoma State University and built a respected career marked by professionalism, responsibility, and a steady leadership style. He was known for his integrity, calm presence, and thoughtful approach to his work. He was an avid golfer and a longtime member of Hacienda Golf Club, where he also served as a board member. He valued the friendships, camaraderie, and community he found there. John was preceded in death by his parents, Charlie King and Juanita May King. He is survived by his wife, Allis King, his daughters Megan Branagh and her husband John, and Whitney Dugan and her husband Adam, and his grandchildren Grady, Henry, Wesley, and Georgie Branagh, and Oliver and Charlie Dugan. He loved his wife deeply and was known by many as a genuine and easygoing friend. A Celebration of Life will be held on November 23, 2025, in La Habra Heights, California. The family invites friends and loved ones to join in honoring his mem']

Name: 4_passed away_doris_grandmother_funeral
Representative_Docs: ['Elaine Jimenez Alvarado Birth: 1967 Death: 2025 Elaine Jimenez Alvarado, age 58, of Santa Ana, California, passed away peacefully on November 11, 2025. Born on August 23, 1967, at St. Joseph Hospital in Orange, CA, Elaine was the beloved daughter of Gilbert and Eustolia Alvarado. Elaine spent her entire life in Santa Ana, where she was a member of the Floral Park community. She dedicated 40 years to the Orange County Register/Freedom Communications Packaging Department, beginning her career as a teenager. Elaine took great pride in her work and loved her job and her coworkers. Elaine was a loving daughter, sister, aunt, and friend. She is survived by her sisters, Annette Tellez and Sandra Rocha; her brothers, Gilbert Alvarado, Mike Alvarado, Albert Alvarado, John Alvarado, and Harvey Alvarado; 11 nephews; 7 nieces; 8 in-laws; and a large extended family who adored her. She was preceded in death by her parents and her older sister, Helga Torres. Known for her generosity, kindness, and beautiful spirit, Elaine touched the hearts of all who knew her. Her gentle presence, loving nature, and unwavering support for her family and friends made her a precious gift to them. She will be deeply missed, and her memor', 'Jane Marie Solaro Birth: 1934 Death: 2026 Jane passed away peacefully on January 4, 2026 at the age of 91. Jane was born in Pacific Grove, CA on December 14, 1934 and lived in San Pedro her entire life. She is preceded in death by her loving husband John Solaro and her parents Matthew Bodul & Ann (Berkovich) Bodul. Jane enjoyed the last four years at the Canterbury in Rancho Palos Verdes with her loving friends and caretakers. She is survived by her loving children, Karen Solaro Durber (Martin), Denise Solaro and John Solaro (Lynn). She was a loving grandmother to Anthony Durber (Christina), Michelle Durber Dilly (Ian), Mark Durber, Nikki Solaro, Ali Solaro Sandoval (Dustin), Jack Solaro and Max Solaro and her great grandchildren Brooklynn and Logan Dilly She is also survived by her siblings Bob Bodul (Marylou), Sharon Bodul Werth (Fred) and Matthew Bodul (Angie) as well as several nieces and nephews. She will be deeply missed and remembered by all. Graveside burial at Green Hills Cemetery on Friday, January 23, 2026 @ 2 pm. Celebration of Life to follow. Honor with Flowers In memory of Jane Marie Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Jane Marie', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

For big parts of the United States, the past few days have brought lots of snow and lots of cold. Where there's snow and cold, there are slips, falls and ice-related injuries. Ice forms along the shore of Lake Michigan on Jan. 23 in Chicago. Name: 6_laboratory_nurses_hospital_patients
Representative_Docs: ['HNL Lab Medicine Brings Escape Room Fun to Hospital Education ALLENTOWN, Pa., Nov. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- HNL Lab Medicine is transforming hospital training by turning mandatory education into an adventure. To strengthen the collaboration between laboratory and nursing teams, HNL Lab Medicine\'s Clinical Hospital Advisors have introduced an innovative "escape room"–style educational game that turns essential lab‐related lessons into an interactive experience. The escape room begins with a familiar challenge: how to avoid getting a phone call from the lab about a rejected sample. From there, nurses progress through a series of puzzles based on real‐world scenarios, selecting properly filled blood culture bottles; answering type & screen questions; identifying best practices to prevent clotted or hemolyzed samples; ordering laboratory tubes correctly, and collecting laboratory tubes in the correct order of draw. Each correct answer provides a key or code to unlock the next stage, ultimately leading to a prize. The activity takes less than ten minutes per nurse, no longer than a standard training session, but delivers far more excitement and engagement. The format encourages problem‐solving, sparks conversation, and helps key lessons stick. Name: 7_family_family members_gifts_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: Family, friends don’t seem to appreciate time I put into tedious Christmas cookie tradition Dear Eric: My late mother made a special Christmas cookie that required tedious decorating, considering each person’s personal preference for toppings, and giving them as gifts to family and friends. They always had a place of honor on our family Christmas table. When she was no longer able, she gave me the cookie cutter, and my daughters and I took over the family tradition. It takes the three of us several days to make, bake, decorate, package and mail cookies to family members around the country. We enjoy doing it and feel like we’re continuing the fun and festive tradition. However, in recent years, not all of the recipients acknowledge receipt. At some point in their lives, they have all helped Grandma decorate, so they know the time involved. Because of past delivery issues, I track the boxes and inquire if I haven’t heard from them in a couple of days. Last year, one response was “yes we got them.” This feels unappreciative! In the past most would text a picture of their children with the cookies, which we really enjoyed. We rarely see any of them anymore, as they are busy raising their families and don’t live nearby. This seems like a way for us to continue somewhat of a co', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Name: 8_cricket_opening match_injury_world cup
Representative_Docs: ['Australia’s Hazlewood unlikely to be fit for half of World Cup, Cummins says Australian fast bowler Josh Hazlewood may not return to action until halfway through the T20 World Cup, injured teammate Pat Cummins said on Tuesday. Hazlewood, arguably the squad’s most important bowler, has struggled with hamstring and Achilles injuries and missed the entire five-Test Ashes series against England. Australia head coach Andrew McDonald said in mid-January Hazlewood was on track to be fit for the squad’s World Cup opener against Ireland on February 11, but Cummins’s update was less optimistic. “I caught up with Joshy a few days ago, and he’s working so hard. I really feel for Joshy. He’s had a few setbacks. [He’s] one of the hardest trainers,” Cummins told Australian broadcaster Nine Network. “He’s doing everything he can. I don’t think he will be there for the start of the tournament, but he’s trying his best to be there about halfway through.” With Mitchell Starc having retired from the format and Cummins sidelined with back problems, Australia head into the February 7 to March 8 World Cup undermanned and in poor form after a 111-run humiliation by Pakistan, which completed a 3-0 series whitewash. Cummins said players’ late arrival to Pakistan after the domestic B', 'In-form England must keep developing to have Six Nations chance, says George By Mitch Phillips England hooker and former captain Jamie George has welcomed the confidence swirling around the team going into the Six Nations but said they cannot expect to win the title by playing the same way as they did to build an 11-match winning streak last year. England last won the tournament in 2020, and their most recent Grand Slam was in 2016, both under Eddie Jones, but they go into their opening match at home to Wales on February 7 with more confidence that they can top the standings than for many years. After narrowly losing their championship opener to Ireland in Dublin almost a year ago, England have chalked up 11 successive wins, including a rare one against New Zealand, and coach Steve Borthwick has built considerable depth in his squad. “We welcome the expectation because it’s a compliment for what we’ve done and what we’ve achieved over a short period of time, but it also keeps us on our toes because we know we can’t be complacent and can’t rely on previous performances,” George said at the Six Nations launch in Edinburgh on Monday. Name: 11_travel_tourism_world cup_events
Representative_Docs: ['Sporting And Cultural Events Boost Travel Intentions For 2026 Photo Credit: Deposit Photos Thirty-four percent of American travelers say they are likely to take an overnight trip to attend a sporting event in 2026. Twenty percent plan to attend a FIFA World Cup match, and 52% intend to travel overnight for cultural events such as concerts and festivals. Meanwhile, 47% are interested in touring Route 66 during its 100th anniversary year. These figures highlight a shift for the coming year where travel decisions are increasingly driven by events rather than relaxation. In 2026, Americans are choosing what to attend, not just where to go. The latest Longwoods International study shows that milestone celebrations and global competitions are now major motivators for travel. The calendar is driving demand. “Special events such as the FIFA World Cup, America 250 and the 100th anniversary of Route 66 will all motivate U.S. travel this year,” said Amir Eylon, President and CEO of Longwoods International. The industry has emphasized experience-based travel, but 2026 denotes a more specific trend. Events offer deadlines, shared participation, and reasons to plan trips in advance. The FIFA World Cup brings global visibility and international visitors. Name: 12_monitors_tvs_monitor_display
Representative_Docs: ['Luckfox 16-inch 4K 120Hz portable touch monitor offers 100% sRGB coverage The segment of portable monitors continues to grow, with many models available on Amazon at relatively affordable prices, typically between $100 and $150. These screens can be useful for productivity on business trips or while traveling. But portable monitors that combine a high resolution with a fast refresh rate remain uncommon in the mid-range segment. Luckfox wants to address this gap with its new 16" 4K Touch Monitor. The 16-inch display features a 3840 × 2400 (WQUXGA) IPS panel with 350 nits brightness and a 1200:1 contrast ratio. It uses optical bonding technology with 6H-hardness toughened glass and a matte finish to minimize ambient reflections. The 16:10 aspect ratio provides more vertical workspace than the standard 16:9 format. It also supports a 120Hz refresh rate, which is an upgrade over the common 60Hz panels found in many portable monitors, such as the Auzai ME16Z01 or the Arzopa A1M; response times aren\'t disclosed, though. As for connectivity, the product integrates two USB Type-C ports and a Mini HDMI input. Both USB-C ports support audio, video, and power delivery, meaning that only a single USB-C cable is required when connected to a compatible smartphone, tab', 'Samsung OLED 48S95H: New 48-inch and 165 Hz OLED TV revealed ahead of schedule with HDR10 Plus Advanced support Samsung has already teased plenty of devices on the eve of CES 2026. However, the trade show has decided to lift the lid on one of Samsung\'s forthcoming OLED Smart TVs. For context, Samsung listed numerous unreleased Smart TVs on its Parts Database over a month ago. Of those, CES has announced that the S95H is a 2026 Honoree in its Gaming & eSports category. Specifically, the trade show has signalled out a 48-inch variant that was missing from November\'s leak. Incidentally, last year\'s S95F only went as small as 55-inches (curr. $1,997 on Amazon). According to the listing, the S95H offers a 165 Hz refresh rate with VRR technology supported. Assuming a 4K resolution from the S95F series and the usage of HDMI 2.1 ports, a 165 Hz refresh rate would require the usage of Display Stream Compression (DSC) to output at its peak resolution and refresh rate simultaneously. Moreover, the new 48-inch Smart TV will support Samsung\'s new HDR10 Plus Advanced format. Pricing and availability are unknown for now; the full description provided by Samsung and CES is as follows: The Samsung OLED (S95H) is the world\'s most advanced 48" OLED TV for high-end gaming. Combining unparalleled brightness and', "World's largest monitor company designed a 4K 48-inch OLED dual-sided smart gaming display with metal feet, a magnetic power socket, and an Alienware-inspired sound system - but who will actually use it? Sign up for breaking news, reviews, opinion, top tech deals, and more. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful GP TV has unveiled what it calls the world’s first dual-sided 48-inch 4K OLED television, designed for gaming and sports viewing. The display is intended to deliver immersive experiences on both sides simultaneously, with built-in audio and dynamic lighting effects. TPV Technology Group, the company behind GP TV, claims the screen targets premium users who value high-end visual fidelity, as well as the public sector and government institutions. This design feels unusual because most monitors or televisions sit against a wall or on a desk, making a rear-facing display largely unnecessary. Its relevance only emerges if the screen stands in the center of a room, similar to display setups commonly seen in shopping malls - and even then, deploying such a device in public spaces would increase the risk of physical damage, leaving the practical use case for this product uncertain. It comes with an audio system that integrates surround sound, Dolby Atmos support, and sound tracking that follows on-screen action. Speakers with fabric covers on both sides creat"]

Name: 13_museum_egypt_egyptian_world largest
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 14_new film_actor_sequel_director
Representative_Docs: ["AP Entertainment SummaryBrief at 3:08 p.m. EST Brendan Fraser's new horizons include 'Rental Family' and rental hedgehogs BEDFORD, N.Y. (AP) — “Rental Family” is Brendan Fraser’s first leading role since winning the best actor Oscar for “The Whale.” In it, the 56-year-old plays a struggling actor living in Japan who, out of desperation and curiosity, takes a job with an agency that hires out actors to fulfill roles in real people’s lives. In an interview, Fraser discusses his life since the Oscar win, his eye-opening experiences in Japan and what returning to the “Mummy” series means to him. His time in Japan included a visit to a hedgehog cafe. “Rental Family” opens Friday in theaters. Copyright 2025 The Associated Press. All rights reserved. Name: 15_hockey_winter olympics_olympics_olympic
Representative_Docs: ['Heartbreak for Canada, Sidney Crosby in Olympic hockey final MILAN -- For a nation that measures itself in hockey gold, Sunday\'s heartbreaking 2-1 defeat to the United States in overtime was more than a lost final. It was a symbolic shift in a rivalry Canada has long considered its own to win. Canada, owners of nine Olympic men\'s hockey titles to the Americans\' three, is a country whose national identity is intertwined with the sport. This one will sting. "Losing hurts, doesn\'t matter who it\'s to," Canada\'s coach Jon Cooper said. "I look at this game, I\'m so proud of what went on. I\'m proud of our country. I\'m proud of our players and proud of our team. "At the end, you\'re going to read in a book that in 2026 USA won the gold in Olympic hockey and Canada won the silver. I think in the grand scheme, the big winner of this tournament was ice hockey." SHADOW OF CROSBY\'S ABSENCE Back home, bleary-eyed Canadians had nursed beers before breakfast, bars opening as early as 5 a.m. on the West Coast as a nation gathered in hope. Instead, they watched the U.S. celebrating, and Canada left to swallow a rare heartbreak on hockey\'s biggest stage, and on the heels of Canada\'s women\'s team losing gold to the U.S. in overtime. The absence of captain Sidney', 'U.S. Women’s Hockey Team declines Trump invite to State of the Union The U.S. Women’s Hockey Team declined an invite to President Donald Trump‘s State of the Union address on Monday, shortly after a clip of Trump making light of the women’s team made the rounds. Both American squads won gold at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan, taking down the Canadian team in their respective finals. On Sunday, Trump invited the men’s team to his annual speech and joked about the inclusion of the women’s team. “I must tell you, we’re going to have to bring the women’s team, you do know that,” Trump said, adding that he “probably would be impeached” if he didn’t. Everything about this is so genuinely gross but the “I guess I have to invite the women’s team too or else I’ll get impeached” is fucking crazy. Britta Curl is that your boy? Ice Hockey team. THEY WON THE GOLD. WOW!” he wrote. When the women’s team won just days earlier, Trump remained silent. Start your day with essential news from Salon. Name: 16_immigration_britain_immigrants_diversity
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Proportions were similar when']

There isn’t a The Morning Show Season 4 Episode 11 release date and time. This is because the season has ended with just 10 episodes, according to the show’s official schedule. The series features a stellar cast that includes Jennifer Aniston, Reese Witherspoon, and Billy Crudup, among others. Yes, The Morning Show Season 4 has finished. The fourth season of the show released its 10th and final episode, titled “Knowing Violation,” recently. There won’t be an additional episode in the coming weeks. However, fans need not worry as it will come back with a new season. In September 2025, Apple TV+ announced that The Morning Show had been renewed for Season 5. But a release window has yet to be confirmed. You can watch The Morning Show Season 4 via Apple TV+. Name: 18_welfare_wages_labour_social security
Representative_Docs: ['How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Benefits Budget 2025 Child Benefit Department for Work and Pensions DWP Pensions PIP Rachel Reeves Universal Credit National government Personal Finance DWP benefits including Universal Credit, Child Benefit, PIP, Carer\'s Allowance, and the State Pension are to increase in April 2026 after Rachel Reeves announced higher payments in her budget speech today. Anyone who claims DWP benefits should be in line for an increase in the next financial year, as the chancellor laid out plans to help pensioners, disabled people, parents, low earners, carers and unemployed people keep up with rising costs. Addressing MPs, Ms Reeves said: "The broken welfare system that we inherited wrote off millions of people as too sick to work and we will reform that system so that it is a system that does not count the cost of failure, but one that protects people who cannot work and empowers those who can. "We have brought back face-to-face assessments for disability benefits. Those are the face-to-face assessments that the shadow chancellor (Sir Mel Stride) got rid of when he was work and pensions secretary and our changes we have made to Universal Credit will get 15,000 people back into work, a figure con', 'How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Name: 19_federal reserve_florence_meeting_economy
Representative_Docs: ['Florence Press Conference Confirms ECB Remains Comfortable in Its Good Place Even a change of scenery has done nothing to change the European Central Bank’s current wait-and-see stance. Florence, where the ECB met today for its only annual meeting outside of Frankfurt, is apparently also this ’good place’, the central bank’s well-known pseudonym for comfortably keeping interest rates on hold. ECB President Christine Lagarde even called it a ‘magnificent’ place – Florence, that is, not rates. The comments at the press conference confirmed once again that the ECB has no intention to leave its ‘good place’ any time soon. We had feared a rather uneventful press conference. What should we say? We hate to be right… Anyhow, here are a few interesting remarks made by Lagarde: On the ECB’s risk assessment, the introductory statement had the most remarkable change compared with September. Last month, the ECB had still talked about risks to the growth outlook that had become “more balanced”; today, it refrained from qualifying any balance of risk. Instead, the central bank had a somewhat more extensive list of upside and downside risks to the growth outlook. Lagarde even said that some of the downside risks had abated. Name: 20_surgical_hospital_memorial hospital_patients
Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques.
